LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS DANIEL BALSAM, CATHY RILEY, KRISTINA KIRBY AND ANGELA BRIDGES' DEMURRER TO AMENDED ANSWER TO COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE, SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SECOND AND THIRD AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES AND SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FOURTH AND FIFTH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES. THE FIRST AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE IS STANDARD PLEADING. THE SECOND AND THIRD AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES SHOULD IDENTIFY THE "THIRD PARTIES" AND THE "APPLICABLE LAWS." THE FOURTH AND FIFTH PURPORTED AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES ARE NOT ACTUALLY AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES. AMENDED ANSWER TO BE FILED BY JANUARY 13, 2012. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: SONIA WONG, CSR #11288
ORDER SUSTAINING DEMURRER TO DEFENDANTS' AMENDED ANSWER TO VERIFIED COMPLAINT

DEMURRER TO AMENDED ANSWER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-16-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS DANIEL BALSAM, CATHY RILEY, KRISTINA KIRBY AND ANGELA BRIDGES' DEMURRER TO AMENDED ANSWER TO COMPLAINT.
Appearances: Eileen M. Shern of Willenken Wilson Loh & Lieb LLP (213.955.8025) for defendants and responding parties; Daniel Balsam of the Law Offices of Daniel Balsam (869.2873) for plaintiffs and moving parties.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: overruled as to the first affirmative defense, sustained with leave to amend as to the second and third affirmative defenses and sustained without leave to amend as to the fourth and fifth affirmative defenses. The first affirmative defense is standard pleading. The second and third affirmative defenses should identify the "third parties" and the "applicable laws." The fourth and fifth purported affirmative defenses are not actually affirmative defenses. Amended answer to be filed by January 13, 2012. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Sonia Wong, CSR #11288
